text:
EXAMINATION: Ga-68 PSMA-11 PET/CT, whole body with diagnostic CT, without IV contrast

INDICATION: 72-year-old male with metastatic castration-resistant prostatic adenocarcinoma, Gleason 9 (4+5), germline BRCA2 mutation. Prior treatment history includes ADT with abi/prednisone (discontinued at CRPC transition), Xtandi (discontinued at radiographic progression with new thoracic vertebral and rib metastases), followed by Lynparza 300 mg BID reduced to 200 mg BID for grade 3 anemia. Presenting now with rising PSA (18.4 ng/mL), new hepatic hypodense lesions and pulmonary nodules on recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, and worsening performance status. Study requested to characterize extent of PSMA-avid disease and evaluate eligibility for lutetium-177 PSMA-targeted therapy.

TECHNIQUE: 370 MBq of Ga-68 PSMA-11 administered intravenously. PET acquisition performed approximately 60 minutes post-injection from the vertex to proximal thighs, with simultaneous low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic coregistration. No IV contrast administered (patient declined). Images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes with fused PET/CT display. Background liver SUVmean 4.9, spleen SUVmean 6.1 (reference values for this scan).

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
No pathologically PSMA-avid cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary lymphadenopathy. Salivary glands demonstrate physiologic PSMA uptake bilaterally (submandibular SUVmax 18.3, parotid SUVmax 10.1), as expected. No intracranial lesions on the CT attenuation correction images; dedicated brain imaging not performed.

CHEST:
Lungs: Right lower lobe peripheral pulmonary nodule, 8 mm, demonstrates mild PSMA avidity (SUVmax 6.2). Left lower lobe nodule, 6 mm, with mild to moderate uptake (SUVmax 4.8). These are new compared to CT from approximately 4 months prior. No additional pulmonary nodules identified with pathologic PSMA avidity. No pleural effusion. No hilar or mediastinal PSMA-avid adenopathy.

Thoracic Spine / Chest Wall: Thoracic vertebral lesions at T10 and T12 demonstrate relatively muted PSMA uptake consistent with post-radiation effect (prior EBRT 20 Gy/5 fractions); SUVmax T10 2.8, T12 2.4. Right 4th rib lesion demonstrates residual low-level uptake (SUVmax 3.1). New foci of moderate PSMA uptake in T7 (SUVmax 7.8) and T9 (SUVmax 6.4) vertebral bodies, not previously targeted with radiation, consistent with new osseous metastatic lesions at these levels. Corresponding sclerotic/lytic changes on CT at T7 and T9.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: Multiple hepatic lesions with elevated PSMA uptake identified throughout both lobes. At least four discrete lesions are appreciable:
  - Segment 5 lesion: 1.9 cm (slightly enlarged from 1.8 cm on prior CT), SUVmax 12.4. This corresponds to the previously biopsied site confirmed as metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma.
  - Segment 7 lesion: 1.4 cm (from 1.2 cm on prior CT), SUVmax 9.8.
  - Segment 6 lesion: 1.1 cm, new, SUVmax 8.3.
  - Segment 4b lesion: 0.9 cm, new, SUVmax 7.6.
Overall hepatic disease burden has increased substantially compared to prior imaging.

Spleen, Pancreas, Adrenal Glands: No PSMA-avid lesions. Physiologic adrenal and splenic uptake. Pancreas not well visualized on low-dose CT but no focal PSMA uptake.

Kidneys/Urinary Tract: Physiologic radiotracer excretion through the renal collecting systems and bladder, limiting assessment of adjacent structures. No focal renal PSMA-avid lesions beyond expected physiologic activity. Bladder moderately filled with tracer.

PELVIS AND RETROPERITONEUM:
Lymph nodes: Right obturator lymph node measuring approximately 2.0 cm in short axis with increased PSMA uptake compared to prior imaging (SUVmax 14.2; previously low avidity on PSMA PET performed approximately 8 months ago when ola was initiated). Right external iliac node, 1.3 cm, SUVmax 9.7. Left external iliac chain: at least one 8 mm node with faint but above-background PSMA avidity (SUVmax 3.8). No iliac or para-aortic adenopathy above the aortic bifurcation with definite pathologic PSMA avidity, though a 7 mm right common iliac node is borderline (SUVmax 2.9, indeterminate).

Prostate Bed: The prostate gland demonstrates heterogeneous uptake in keeping with the known primary tumor; no post-surgical changes. No seminal vesicle PSMA avidity beyond prior known disease extent.

OSSEOUS METASTASES (PELVIS AND LOWER EXTREMITIES):
Extensive skeletal metastatic disease with PSMA-avid lesions throughout. Compared to the PSMA PET from approximately 8 months prior (which demonstrated substantial metabolic response to ola), there is now clearly increased PSMA avidity in multiple lesions, suggesting interval progression:
  - Lumbar spine: L2, L3, L4 all demonstrate increased PSMA uptake (L2 SUVmax 18.6, L3 SUVmax 15.4, L4 SUVmax 12.1). These lesions were previously less avid on the prior PSMA PET.
  - Sacroiliac joints: Bilateral increased uptake, left greater than right (left sacral ala SUVmax 11.3, right sacroiliac joint SUVmax 8.9).
  - Right iliac wing: Expansile sclerotic lesion with intense uptake (SUVmax 16.7).
  - Right femoral neck and proximal shaft: Increased avidity (SUVmax 13.2), cortical thickening visible on CT correlate.
  - New focal uptake in left posterior iliac crest not previously identified (SUVmax 5.4).

IMPRESSION:

1. Markedly widespread PSMA-avid disease with clear evidence of progression across multiple disease compartments compared to the prior PSMA PET study performed approximately 8 months ago.

2. Liver: At least four PSMA-avid hepatic lesions throughout the right and left lobes, with the largest demonstrating an SUVmax of 12.4. Two lesions are new since the prior CT. All lesions exceed background liver uptake substantially, in keeping with active metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma.

3. Lungs: Both previously identified pulmonary nodules (right and left lower lobes) demonstrate definite PSMA avidity above background, supporting metastatic involvement rather than benign etiology.

4. Pelvic lymph nodes: Right obturator and external iliac nodes have markedly increased PSMA uptake compared to prior study, with the obturator node now among the most avid non-osseous sites. Additional left-sided nodal disease cannot be excluded.

5. Osseous metastases: Extensive and progressing throughout the lumbar spine, sacroiliac joints, pelvis, and right proximal femur. Post-radiation changes at T10 and T12 are consistent with treatment effect. New thoracic vertebral lesions at T7 and T9 with moderate uptake identified. A new left posterior iliac crest focus is suspicious for an additional osseous site.

6. The breadth and intensity of PSMA expression across visceral, nodal, and osseous disease sites supports eligibility for PSMA-directed radioligand therapy from an imaging standpoint, with no PSMA-negative dominant lesions identified that would suggest heterogeneous PSMA expression patterns precluding this approach.
